Strategy 2.name: wages_s2
Strategy 2.logic: Renamed from HahnasaSahar. Missing values replaced with 0.
Strategy 2.code:
# Rename CBS variable
tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21.rename(
    columns={'HahnasaSahar': 'wages_s2'},
    inplace=True
)

# Convert missing values to 0
tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['wages_s2'] = (
    tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['wages_s2'].fillna(0)
)

Strategy 2.name: business_income_s2
Strategy 2.logic: Renamed from Hahnasa_iskit. Missing values replaced with 0.
Strategy 2.code:
# Rename CBS variable
tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21.rename(
    columns={'Hahnasa_iskit': 'business_income_s2'},
    inplace=True
)

# Convert missing values to 0
tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['business_income_s2'] = (
    tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['business_income_s2'].fillna(0)
)

Strategy 2.name: capital_gains_s2
Strategy 2.logic: Renamed from Hahnasa_honit. Missing values replaced with 0.
Strategy 2.code:
# Rename CBS variable
tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21.rename(
    columns={'Hahnasa_honit': 'capital_gains_s2'},
    inplace=True
)

# Convert missing values to 0
tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['capital_gains_s2'] = (
    tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['capital_gains_s2'].fillna(0)
)

Strategy 2.name: dividends_s2
Strategy 2.logic: Renamed from Hahnasa_honit_aheret. Missing values replaced with 0.

Strategy 2.name: other_wage_s2
Strategy 2.definition: Labor income from a former employment relation (e.g., pension)
Strategy 2.logic: Renamed from hahnasa_aheret. Missing values replaced with 0.

Strategy 2.name: selfowned_income_s2
Strategy 2.definition: Income received directly from a self-owned business
Strategy 2.logic: RevahGolmi for individuals whose SugTikShatMas (tax file type) is one of: 10, 20, 40, 41, 42, 43, 52, 53, 91, 96, 98. These codes identify self-employed business owners filing specific tax form types.

Strategy 2.name: property_income_s2
Strategy 2.definition: Property income: dividends, rent, and interest
Strategy 2.logic: Sum of HahnasaHonitAheret (other capital income including dividends) and HahnasaLeLoYilshit (tax-exempt income, primarily rent and interest).

Strategy 2.name: tax_report
Strategy 2.definition: Binary variable: whether individual filed a tax report (form 1301) versus being reported only in employer form 106
Strategy 2.logic: Set to 1 if any of the following 8 variables is greater than 0: Hahnasa_Aheret, HahnasaHonit, HahnasaHonitAheret, HahnasaIskit, HahnasaLeLoYilshit, HahnasaNehe, HachnasaPtura, RevahGolmi. Set to 0 otherwise. Distinguishes tax filers from wage-only individuals.

Strategy 2.name: labor_income_s2
Strategy 2.definition: Total labor income (wages and labor income from former employment)
Strategy 2.logic: Sum of wages_s2 (HahnasaSahar) and other_wage_s2 (Hahnasa_Aheret, e.g., pension from former employment). Missing values handled with min_count=1.

Strategy 2.name: capital_income_s2
Strategy 2.definition: Total capital income (self-owned business + property + capital gains)
Strategy 2.logic: Sum of selfowned_income_s2, property_income_s2, and capital_gains_s2.

Strategy 2.name: total_income_s2
Strategy 2.logic: Sum of labor_income_s2, capital_income_s2, and business_income_s2.

Strategy 2.name: total_income_r1_s2
Strategy 2.definition: Total income variant r1 (wages + capital + business, excluding other labor income)
Strategy 2.logic: Sum of wages_s2, capital_income_s2, and business_income_s2. Uses wages_s2 instead of labor_income_s2, excluding other_wage_s2.

Strategy 2.name: capital_income_r1_s2
Strategy 2.definition: Capital income variant r1 (property + capital gains only, excluding self-owned business)
Strategy 2.logic: Sum of property_income_s2 and capital_gains_s2. Excludes selfowned_income_s2.

Strategy 2.name: total_income_r2_s2
Strategy 2.definition: Total income variant r2 (wages + capital_r1 + business)
Strategy 2.logic: Sum of wages_s2, capital_income_r1_s2, and business_income_s2. Uses capital_income_r1_s2 which excludes self-owned business income.

Strategy 3.name: benefits_allocated_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Benefits allocated from national accounts to individuals (fringe benefits)
Strategy 3.logic: Fringe benefits at the national level equal labor compensation minus employers' social contribution to national insurance and health tax minus all individual taxed wages minus other labor income. Amount from national accounts Row 52 (in million NIS). Allocated to individuals according to the distribution of wages_s2.

Strategy 3.name: benefits_allocated_r1_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Benefits allocated from national accounts (alternative: labor compensation less social contributions less wages)
Strategy 3.logic: Fringe benefits at the national level equal labor compensation less employers' social contribution to national insurance and health tax less all individual taxed wages. Amount from national accounts Row n.45 (in million NIS). Allocated to individuals according to the distribution of wages_s2.

Strategy 3.name: labor_income_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total labor income including allocated benefits
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of labor_income_s2 and benefits_allocated_s3.

Strategy 3.name: labor_income_r1_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Labor income variant r1 (wages + alternative benefits allocation)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of wages_s2 and benefits_allocated_r1_s3.

Strategy 3.name: property_reported_income_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total reported property income (property + capital gains)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of property_income_s2 and capital_gains_s2.

Strategy 3.name: property_attributed_rent_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Tax-exempt housing rent attributed from national accounts
Strategy 3.logic: Tax-exempt housing rent. Amount from national accounts Row n.8 (in million NIS). Distributed among all individuals according to the distribution of property_reported_income_s3 among individuals with property_reported_income_s3 > 0.

Strategy 3.name: property_attributed_income_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Taxable property income attributed to non-filers from national accounts
Strategy 3.logic: Taxable property income of employees who are not required to submit an annual tax report. Amount from national accounts Row n.22 (in million NIS). Distributed among individuals with tax_report=0 according to the distribution of property_reported_income_s3 among individuals with tax_report=1.

Strategy 3.name: property_income_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total property income (reported + attributed rent)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of property_reported_income_s3 and property_attributed_rent_s3.

Strategy 3.name: property_income_r1_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total property income variant r1 (reported + attributed rent + attributed income)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of property_reported_income_s3, property_attributed_rent_s3, and property_attributed_income_s3.

Strategy 3.name: profits_attributed_income_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Business profits attributed from national accounts
Strategy 3.logic: Attributed business income (business_income_s2). Amount from national accounts Row n.28 (in million NIS). Distributed among all individuals according to the distribution of property_reported_income_s3 among individuals with property_reported_income_s3 > 0.

Strategy 3.name: capital_income_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total capital income (property + attributed profits + self-owned)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of property_income_s3, profits_attributed_income_s3, and selfowned_income_s2.

Strategy 3.name: capital_income_r1_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Capital income variant r1 (property + attributed profits, excluding self-owned)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of property_income_s3 and profits_attributed_income_s3.

Strategy 3.name: capital_income_r2_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Capital income variant r2 (property_r1 + attributed profits)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of property_income_r1_s3 and profits_attributed_income_s3.

Strategy 3.name: income_nonfilers_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Imputed income for non-filers (30% of average total_income_r1_s2)
Strategy 3.logic: Set to 0.3 multiplied by the average of total_income_r1_s2. Applied to non-filer observations added from population aggregates.

Strategy 3.name: total_income_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total income (labor + capital + business + non-filer imputation)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of labor_income_s3, capital_income_s3, business_income_s2, and income_nonfilers_s3.

Strategy 3.name: total_income_r1_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total income variant r1 (labor_r1 + capital + business + non-filers)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of labor_income_r1_s3, capital_income_s3, business_income_s2, and income_nonfilers_s3.

Strategy 3.name: total_income_r4_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total income variant r4 (labor_r1 + property + business)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of labor_income_r1_s3, property_income_s3, and business_income_s2.

Strategy 3.name: total_income_r5_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total income variant r5 (labor_r1 + property + business + attributed profits)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of labor_income_r1_s3, property_income_s3, business_income_s2, and profits_attributed_income_s3.

Strategy 3.name: income_nonfilers_r1_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Imputed income for non-filers variant r1 (30% of average total_income_r4_s3)
Strategy 3.logic: Set to 0.3 multiplied by the average of total_income_r4_s3.

Strategy 3.name: total_income_r6_s3
Strategy 3.definition: Total income variant r6 (labor_r1 + property + business + profits + non-filers_r1)
Strategy 3.logic: Sum of labor_income_r1_s3, property_income_s3, business_income_s2, profits_attributed_income_s3, and income_nonfilers_r1_s3.

Strategy 4.name: firm_id
Strategy 4.definition: Firm identifier (actual workplace ID with industry fallback)
Strategy 4.logic: Coalesce of koteret_mis_tik_fic (actual workplace tax file ID) and pseudo_firm2 (industry 2-digit code). Uses the actual firm ID when available, falling back to industry code when the firm ID is missing.

Strategy 4.name: firm_size
Strategy 4.definition: Number of workers per firm per year
Strategy 4.logic: Count of individual IDs (MisparTeudatZehut_fic) grouped by firm_id. Gives the number of employees in each firm for that year. Used to distinguish small firms (<20) from large firms for profit distribution.

Strategy 4.name: hafra_oved_kitsva
Strategy 4.definition: Amount of employee contributions to a comprehensive pension
Strategy 4.logic: CBS source variable (no transformation).
Strategy 4.code: # CBS source variable — used directly from tbl_misrot27_21

Strategy 4.name: hafra_oved_ovdan
Strategy 4.definition: Amount of employee contributions for disability insurance
Strategy 4.logic: CBS source variable (no transformation).
Strategy 4.code: # CBS source variable — used directly from tbl_misrot27_21

Strategy 4.name: hafra_mavid_kitsva
Strategy 4.definition: Amount of employer contributions to a comprehensive pension
Strategy 4.logic: CBS source variable (no transformation).
Strategy 4.code: # CBS source variable — used directly from tbl_misrot27_21

Strategy 4.name: hafra_mavid_ovdan
Strategy 4.definition: Amount of employer contributions for disability insurance
Strategy 4.logic: CBS source variable (no transformation).
Strategy 4.code: # CBS source variable — used directly from tbl_misrot27_21

Strategy 4.name: pension_employer_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Employer contributions for pension (comprehensive + disability)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of hafra_mavid_kitsva and hafra_mavid_ovdan, each clipped at 0 (negatives removed), missing set to 0.

Strategy 4.name: pension_employee_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Employee contributions for pension (comprehensive + disability)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of hafra_oved_kitsva and hafra_oved_ovdan, each clipped at 0, missing set to 0.

Strategy 4.name: public_sector
Strategy 4.definition: Whether the firm is in the public sector (1) or private (0)
Strategy 4.logic: Set to 1 for specific SectorMeuchad codes (110010, 110015, 121000, 131100, 131110, 131300, 131400, 131500). Set to 0 for private sector codes and range 122010–129030.
Strategy 4.code:
df['public_sector'] = np.nan

public_sector_codes = [
    110010, 110015, 121000, 131100,
    131110, 131300, 131400, 131500
]
df.loc[
    df['SectorMeuchad'].isin(public_sector_codes),
    'public_sector'
] = 1

private_sector_codes = [
    110020, 110021, 110030, 140000,
    150020, 150030, 999999
]
range_condition = (
    (df['SectorMeuchad'] >= 122010)
    & (df['SectorMeuchad'] <= 129030)
)
df.loc[
    df['SectorMeuchad'].isin(private_sector_codes)
    | range_condition,
    'public_sector'
] = 0

Strategy 4.name: tenure
Strategy 4.definition: Years of tenure at current firm
Strategy 4.logic: Current year minus prat_shnat_avodaStart, only when start year is non-missing and >= 0.

Strategy 4.name: pension_budgetary_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Budgetary pension for public sector workers who started before 2004
Strategy 4.logic: 2% × masc_basis_kitsva × tenure, only for public sector workers who started before 2004 and have positive masc_basis_kitsva. Otherwise 0.

Strategy 4.name: pension_all_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Total pension contributions (employer + employee + budgetary)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of pension_employer_s4, pension_employee_s4, pension_budgetary_s4. Missing filled with 0.

Strategy 4.name: hafra_mavid_hish
Strategy 4.definition: Amount of employer contributions to study fund (keren hishtalmut)
Strategy 4.logic: CBS source variable (no transformation).
Strategy 4.code: # CBS source variable — used directly from tbl_misrot27_21

Strategy 4.name: hafra_oved_hish
Strategy 4.definition: Amount of employee contributions to study fund (keren hishtalmut)
Strategy 4.logic: CBS source variable (no transformation).
Strategy 4.code: # CBS source variable — used directly from tbl_misrot27_21

Strategy 4.name: kh_employer_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Employer study fund contributions (keren hishtalmut), cleaned
Strategy 4.logic: hafra_mavid_hish when positive, 0 otherwise.

Strategy 4.name: kh_employee_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Employee study fund contributions (keren hishtalmut), cleaned
Strategy 4.logic: hafra_oved_hish when positive, 0 otherwise.

Strategy 4.name: kh_all_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Total study fund contributions (employer + employee)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of kh_employer_s4 and kh_employee_s4. Missing filled with 0.

Strategy 4.name: benefits_1_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Total employer benefits (pension + study fund)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of pension_all_s4 and kh_all_s4. Missing filled with 0.

Strategy 4.name: benefits_1_allocated_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Allocated benefits: 14% of firm labor costs distributed by wage share
Strategy 4.logic: 14% of labor_costs_leep distributed to workers according to their wage share within the firm (wages_s2 / sum of firm wages).
Strategy 4.code:
df['labor_costs_leep'] = df['labor_costs_leep'].fillna(0)
labor_costs_14_pct = df['labor_costs_leep'] * 0.14

df['benefits_1_allocated_s4'] = 0

sum_wages = df.groupby('firm_id')[
    'wages_s2'
].transform('sum')

df['wage_share'] = df['wages_s2'] / sum_wages

df['benefits_1_allocated_s4'] = (
    df['wage_share'] * labor_costs_14_pct
)
df = df.drop(['wage_share'], axis=1)
df['benefits_1_allocated_s4'] = (
    df['benefits_1_allocated_s4'].fillna(0)
)

Strategy 4.name: benefits_2_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Combined benefits (direct + allocated)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of benefits_1_s4 and benefits_1_allocated_s4. Missing filled with 0.

Strategy 4.name: labor_income_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Total labor income including benefits (wages + benefits)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of wages_s2 and benefits_2_s4. Missing filled with 0.

Strategy 4.name: profits_5_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Distributed firm profits (top-10% earners in large firms, equal in small firms)
Strategy 4.logic: For firms with 20+ workers: profits distributed to top 10% earners by wage share. For firms <20: distributed equally. Self-owned income subtracted.
Strategy 4.code:
df['firm_size'] = df.groupby('firm_id')[
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic'
].transform('count')

small_firm_mask = df['firm_size'] < 20

df['wage_percentile'] = df.groupby('firm_id')[
    'wages_s2'
].transform(lambda x: x.rank(pct=True))

top_10_mask = (
    (df['wage_percentile'] >= 0.9)
    & (~small_firm_mask)
)

df['profits_5_s4'] = 0

# Large firms: top 10% by wage share
sum_top_wages = df[top_10_mask].groupby(
    'firm_id'
)['wages_s2'].transform('sum')
df.loc[top_10_mask, 'wage_share'] = (
    df.loc[top_10_mask, 'wages_s2']
    / sum_top_wages
)
df.loc[top_10_mask, 'profits_5_s4'] = (
    df.loc[top_10_mask, 'wage_share']
    * df.loc[top_10_mask, 'profits_mirsham']
)

# Small firms: equal distribution
df.loc[small_firm_mask, 'profits_5_s4'] = (
    df.loc[small_firm_mask, 'profits_mirsham']
    / df.loc[small_firm_mask, 'firm_size']
)

df['profits_5_s4'] = (
    df['profits_5_s4']
    - df['selfowned_income_s2'].fillna(0)
)
df['profits_5_s4'] = df['profits_5_s4'].fillna(0)

Strategy 4.name: profits_6_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Distributed firm profits (workers with historical positive property income)
Strategy 4.logic: Distributed to current workers with property_income_s3 > 0 and former workers with positive property income history. Profits split equally among eligible workers per firm. Self-owned income subtracted.
Strategy 4.code:
df = create_profits_6_s4(
    df,
    firm_positive_income_property_workers_history,
    year_int
)
df['profits_6_s4'] = (
    df['profits_6_s4']
    - df['selfowned_income_s2'].fillna(0)
)
df['profits_6_s4'] = df['profits_6_s4'].fillna(0)

Strategy 4.name: capital_income_4_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Capital income (property + self-owned business)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of property_income_s3 and selfowned_income_s2.

Strategy 4.name: capital_income_5_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Capital income including distributed profits (property + profits_6 + self-owned)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of property_income_s3, profits_6_s4, and selfowned_income_s2.

Strategy 4.name: total_income_2_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Total income (labor + property + business)
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of labor_income_s4, property_income_s3, and business_income_s2.

Strategy 4.name: income_nonfilers_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Imputed income for non-filers (30% of average total_income_2_s4)
Strategy 4.logic: For rows where added_rows == 1 (non-filers): set to 30% of mean total_income_2_s4. Otherwise 0.
Strategy 4.code:
avg = df['total_income_2_s4'].mean()
df['income_nonfilers_s4'] = 0
df.loc[
    df['added_rows'] == 1,
    'income_nonfilers_s4'
] = avg * 0.3

Strategy 4.name: total_income_5_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Total income including distributed profits
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of labor_income_s4, property_income_s3, business_income_s2, selfowned_income_s2, and profits_6_s4.

Strategy 4.name: total_income_6_s4
Strategy 4.definition: Total income including profits and non-filer imputation
Strategy 4.logic: Sum of labor_income_s4, property_income_s3, business_income_s2, selfowned_income_s2, profits_6_s4, and income_nonfilers_s4.

Production Classes.name: large_proprietor_first_def
Production Classes.definition: Individuals with capital income in more than 5 years (i.e., 6+ years) between 2006 and 2021
Production Classes.logic: For each person-year (2006–2021), flag has_capital_income = 1 if HahnasaHonitAheret > 0 or HahnasaHonit > 0 or HahnasaLeLoYilshit > 0 (NaN filled with 0). Count flagged years per person. Set large_proprietor_first_def = 1 if count > 5. Once classified, the label applies to all years.
Production Classes.code:
tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['has_capital_income'] = (
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['HahnasaHonitAheret'].fillna(0) > 0) |
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['HahnasaHonit'].fillna(0) > 0) |
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['HahnasaLeLoYilshit'].fillna(0) > 0)
).astype(int)

data_filtered = tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21[
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['shana'] >= 2006) &
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['shana'] <= 2021) &
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].notna())
].copy()

years_with_capital = data_filtered.groupby(
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic'
)['has_capital_income'].sum().reset_index()
years_with_capital.columns = [
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic',
    'years_with_capital_income'
]

large_proprietors_ids = years_with_capital[
    years_with_capital['years_with_capital_income'] > 5
]['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].tolist()

social_class_df['large_proprietor_first_def'] = (
    social_class_df['MisparTeudatZehut_fic']
    .isin(large_proprietors_ids).astype(int)
)

Production Classes.name: small_proprietor
Production Classes.definition: Individuals who filed a small-business tax form (SugTikShatMas) in more than 5 years (6+) between 2006 and 2021
Production Classes.logic: For each person-year (2006–2021), flag has_small_business_form = 1 if SugTikShatMas is one of [10, 20, 40, 41, 42, 43, 52, 53, 91, 96, 98]. Count flagged years per person. Set small_proprietor = 1 if count > 5. Once classified, the label applies to all years.
Production Classes.code:
small_business_codes = [
    10, 20, 40, 41, 42, 43, 52, 53, 91, 96, 98
]

data_filtered = tbl_source[
    (tbl_source['shana'] >= 2006) &
    (tbl_source['shana'] <= 2021) &
    (tbl_source['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].notna())
].copy()

data_no_dups = data_filtered.drop_duplicates(
    subset=['MisparTeudatZehut_fic', 'shana'],
    keep='first'
).copy()

data_no_dups['has_small_business_form'] = (
    data_no_dups['SugTikShatMas']
    .isin(small_business_codes).astype(int)
)

years_with_forms = data_no_dups.groupby(
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic'
)['has_small_business_form'].sum().reset_index()
years_with_forms.columns = [
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic',
    'years_with_small_business_forms'
]

small_proprietors_ids = years_with_forms[
    years_with_forms['years_with_small_business_forms'] > 5
]['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].tolist()

small_proprietors_df['small_proprietor'] = (
    small_proprietors_df['MisparTeudatZehut_fic']
    .isin(small_proprietors_ids).astype(int)
)

Production Classes.name: independent_producer
Production Classes.definition: Individuals with self-employment income only (no wages) in more than 5 years (6+) between 1999 and 2021
Production Classes.logic: For each person-year (1999–2021), flag is_independent_producer = 1 if HahnasaIskit is not missing AND HahnasaSaher is missing. Count flagged years per person. Set independent_producer = 1 if count > 5. Once classified, the label applies to all years.
Production Classes.code:
data_filtered = tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21[
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['shana'] >= 1999) &
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['shana'] <= 2021) &
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].notna())
].copy()

data_no_dups = data_filtered.drop_duplicates(
    subset=['MisparTeudatZehut_fic', 'shana'],
    keep='first'
).copy()

data_no_dups['is_independent_producer'] = (
    (data_no_dups['HahnasaIskit'].notna()) &
    (data_no_dups['HahnasaSaher'].isna())
).astype(int)

years_as_independent = data_no_dups.groupby(
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic'
)['is_independent_producer'].sum().reset_index()
years_as_independent.columns = [
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic',
    'years_as_independent_producer'
]

independent_producers_ids = years_as_independent[
    years_as_independent['years_as_independent_producer'] > 5
]['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].tolist()

independent_producers_df['independent_producer'] = (
    independent_producers_df['MisparTeudatZehut_fic']
    .isin(independent_producers_ids).astype(int)
)

Production Classes.name: formal_independent_producer
Production Classes.definition: Independent producers with higher education (BA degree or above)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of independent_producer. Set to 1 if independent_producer == 1 and ba == 1 (completed higher education). Education data merged from tbl_mh2021.

Production Classes.name: informal_independent_producer
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of independent_producer. Set to 1 if independent_producer == 1 and ba == 0 (no higher education). Education data merged from tbl_mh2021.

Production Classes.name: wage_and_independent_producer
Production Classes.definition: Individuals with both wage and self-employment income in more than 5 years (6+) between 1999 and 2021
Production Classes.notebook: Wage and salary workers and Independent producers.ipynb
Production Classes.logic: For each person-year (1999–2021), flag has_mixed_income = 1 if both HahnasaIskit and HahnasaSaher are not missing. Count flagged years per person. Set wage_and_independent_producer = 1 if count > 5. Once classified, the label applies to all years.
Production Classes.code:
data_filtered = tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21[
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['shana'] >= 1999) &
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['shana'] <= 2021) &
    (tbl_hahnsotpratt87_21['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].notna())
].copy()

data_no_dups = data_filtered.drop_duplicates(
    subset=['MisparTeudatZehut_fic', 'shana'],
    keep='first'
).copy()

data_no_dups['has_mixed_income'] = (
    (data_no_dups['HahnasaIskit'].notna()) &
    (data_no_dups['HahnasaSaher'].notna())
).astype(int)

years_with_mixed = data_no_dups.groupby(
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic'
)['has_mixed_income'].sum().reset_index()
years_with_mixed.columns = [
    'MisparTeudatZehut_fic',
    'years_with_mixed_income'
]

mixed_workers_ids = years_with_mixed[
    years_with_mixed['years_with_mixed_income'] > 5
]['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].tolist()

mixed_workers_df['wage_and_independent_producer'] = (
    mixed_workers_df['MisparTeudatZehut_fic']
    .isin(mixed_workers_ids).astype(int)
)

Production Classes.name: wage_and_salary_worker
Production Classes.definition: Individuals with wage income who do not belong to any other production class (2006–2021)
Production Classes.logic: Set to 1 if HahnasaSaher is not missing and the individual is not classified as large_proprietor_first_def, small_proprietor, independent_producer, or wage_and_independent_producer. Period: 2006–2021.

Production Classes.name: manager
Production Classes.definition: Wage workers who are also managers (SugTikShatMas == 30 in tbl_source AND present in tbl_misrot)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of wage_and_salary_worker. Manager has priority over all other sub-classes. Set to 1 if the individual appears in tbl_source with SugTikShatMas == 30 AND appears in tbl_misrot in the same year. Period: 2006–2021.
Production Classes.code:
source_managers = tbl_source[
    (tbl_source['MisparTeudatZehut_fic'].notna()) &
    (tbl_source['SugTikShatMas'] == 30)
][['MisparTeudatZehut_fic', 'shana']].drop_duplicates()
source_managers['in_source_as_manager'] = 1

wage_sub = wage_sub.merge(
    source_managers,
    on=['MisparTeudatZehut_fic', 'shana'],
    how='left'
)
wage_sub['in_source_as_manager'] = (
    wage_sub['in_source_as_manager'].fillna(0).astype(int)
)

wage_sub['manager'] = (
    (wage_sub['in_source_as_manager'] == 1) &
    (wage_sub['in_misrot'] == 1)
).astype(int)

Production Classes.name: gig
Production Classes.definition: Wage workers in nonstandard employment (daily job, daily/hourly worker, or short tenure with long attachment)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of wage_and_salary_worker. Not a manager. Set to 1 if prat_sug_misra == 3 (daily job), or sug_oved in [2, 3] (before 2019), or worked_month <= 5 and tenure >= 2. Period: 2006–2021.
Production Classes.code:
cond_job_type = wage_sub['prat_sug_misra'].isin([3])

cond_worker_type = (
    (wage_sub['shana'] < 2019) &
    (wage_sub['sug_oved'].isin([2, 3]))
)

cond_low_months_high_tenure = (
    (wage_sub['worked_month'] <= 5) &
    (wage_sub['tenure'] >= 2)
)

wage_sub['gig'] = (
    (wage_sub['manager'] != 1) &
    (cond_job_type | cond_worker_type
     | cond_low_months_high_tenure)
).astype(int)

Production Classes.name: standard
Production Classes.definition: Wage workers in standard employment (sole monthly job, 10+ months worked, firm size 20+)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of wage_and_salary_worker. Not a manager or gig. Set to 1 if prat_sug_misra == 1 (sole job) and sug_oved == 1 (monthly worker) and worked_month >= 10 and firm_size >= 20. Period: 2006–2021.

Production Classes.name: gig_standard_mix
Production Classes.definition: Wage workers who are neither manager, gig, nor standard (residual sub-class)
Production Classes.logic: Sub-class of wage_and_salary_worker. Set to 1 if wage_and_salary_worker == 1 and manager != 1 and gig != 1 and standard != 1. Period: 2006–2021.

Production Classes.name: out_of_earned_income
Production Classes.definition: Individuals not classified into any other production class (residual category in merge)
Production Classes.logic: Not a separately computed indicator. In the merge, every person-year starts as 'Out of earned income population' and is overwritten if any class indicator is 1. Those with no indicator remain in this residual category.
Production Classes.code:
# Default — everyone starts here
merged['production_class'] = (
    'Out of earned income population'
)
# Then higher-priority classes overwrite
# in reverse priority order (see production_class)

Production Classes.name: production_class
Production Classes.definition: Final mutually exclusive production class label assigned by priority
Production Classes.logic: Priority-based assignment (highest wins): 1) Large Proprietor, 2) Small Proprietor, 3) Independent Producer – Formal / Informal / Unknown Education, 4) Wage & Independent Producer, 5) Wage Worker – Manager / Gig / Standard / Gig-Standard Mix, 6) Out of earned income population. Applied in reverse order so higher priority overwrites lower.
Production Classes.code:
merged['production_class'] = 'Out of earned income population'

# 5d  Gig/Standard Mix
mask = (merged['wage_and_salary_worker'] == 1) & (merged['gig_standard_mix'] == 1)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage Worker – Gig/Standard Mix'

# 5c  Standard
mask = (merged['wage_and_salary_worker'] == 1) & (merged['standard'] == 1)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage Worker – Standard'

# 5b  Gig
mask = (merged['wage_and_salary_worker'] == 1) & (merged['gig'] == 1)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage Worker – Gig'

# 5a  Manager
mask = (merged['wage_and_salary_worker'] == 1) & (merged['manager'] == 1)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage Worker – Manager'

# 4   Wage & Independent Producer
mask = merged['wage_and_independent_producer'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Wage & Independent Producer'

# 3c  Independent Producer – Unknown Education
mask = (
    (merged['independent_producer'] == 1) &
    (merged['formal_independent_producer'] == 0) &
    (merged['informal_independent_producer'] == 0)
)
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Independent Producer – Unknown Education'

# 3b  Informal
mask = merged['informal_independent_producer'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Independent Producer – Informal'

# 3a  Formal
mask = merged['formal_independent_producer'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Independent Producer – Formal'

# 2   Small Proprietor
mask = merged['small_proprietor'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Small Proprietor'

# 1   Large Proprietor
mask = merged['large_proprietor_first_def'] == 1
merged.loc[mask, 'production_class'] = 'Large Proprietor'

Production Classes.name: production_class_code
Production Classes.definition: Numeric code (1–11) corresponding to the production_class label
Production Classes.logic: Maps production_class text to a numeric code: 1 = Large Proprietor, 2 = Small Proprietor, 3 = Independent Producer – Formal, 4 = Independent Producer – Informal, 5 = Independent Producer – Unknown Education, 6 = Wage & Independent Producer, 7 = Wage Worker – Manager, 8 = Wage Worker – Gig, 9 = Wage Worker – Standard, 10 = Wage Worker – Gig/Standard Mix, 11 = Out of earned income population.
Production Classes.code:
class_codes = {
    'Large Proprietor':                        1,
    'Small Proprietor':                        2,
    'Independent Producer – Formal':           3,
    'Independent Producer – Informal':         4,
    'Independent Producer – Unknown Education': 5,
    'Wage & Independent Producer':             6,
    'Wage Worker – Manager':                   7,
    'Wage Worker – Gig':                       8,
    'Wage Worker – Standard':                  9,
    'Wage Worker – Gig/Standard Mix':         10,
    'Out of earned income population':        11
}

merged['production_class_code'] = (
    merged['production_class'].map(class_codes)
)

Production Classes.name: production_class_broad
Production Classes.logic: Maps production_class to a broader grouping: Independent Producer sub-classes → 'Independent Producer'; Wage Worker sub-classes → 'Wage & Salary Worker'; all others unchanged.
Production Classes.code:
broad_map = {
    'Large Proprietor':                        'Large Proprietor',
    'Small Proprietor':                        'Small Proprietor',
    'Independent Producer – Formal':           'Independent Producer',
    'Independent Producer – Informal':         'Independent Producer',
    'Independent Producer – Unknown Education': 'Independent Producer',
    'Wage & Independent Producer':             'Wage & Independent Producer',
    'Wage Worker – Manager':                   'Wage & Salary Worker',
    'Wage Worker – Gig':                       'Wage & Salary Worker',
    'Wage Worker – Standard':                  'Wage & Salary Worker',
    'Wage Worker – Gig/Standard Mix':          'Wage & Salary Worker',
    'Out of earned income population':         'Out of earned income population'
}

merged['production_class_broad'] = (
    merged['production_class'].map(broad_map)
)
